5 Ways to Maximize Your Take-Home Pay

Because a raise isn’t the only way to boost your income.

1. Maximize Pre-Tax Contributions

401(k), HSA, and FSA contributions reduce your taxable income, which means less tax and more take-home pay. For high earners, this can save thousands annually.

2. Optimize Your Tax Withholding

Update your W-4. Over-withholding means giving the government an interest-free loan. Aim for accuracy, not a big refund.

3. Leverage Employer Benefits

Health, commuter, and dependent care benefits often come out pre-tax. Using them can save 20-30% on expenses you already have.

4. Review Health Insurance Options

Compare plans during open enrollment. High-deductible plans paired with an HSA can save you money if you’re healthy and want to invest extra cash.

5. Avoid Lifestyle Creep

Boosting take-home pay means nothing if you increase spending. Allocate extra cash to savings or debt payoff to accelerate your FI journey.

Pro Tip: Use our Take-Home Pay Calculator to see the real impact of pre-tax contributions and deductions on your paycheck.
